generic.lostYear: Skip it and you forfeit {value} {unit} every year.
generic.lostDecade: A decade off and {decade} {unit} are gone.
generic.yearlyNoUnit: Doing this every day adds up to {days} successful days a year.
generic.lostYearNoUnit: Skip it and you lose {days} successful days a year.
generic.lostDecadeNoUnit: A decade off and you forfeit {decade} successful days.
generic.r1: Start with the smallest possible version of this habit today — even one rep counts.
generic.r2: The body and brain rebuild fast once you give them the right inputs again.

reading.p2: Across a decade, {decadeBooks} books. A small library.
reading.c1: Skip a year and that's {books} books — and the upgraded thinking inside them — that you never get.
reading.c2: A decade of not reading compounds into the same person you are now, only older. Your kids see that, too.
reading.r1: Start with one page tonight before bed — that's it. Tomorrow, two.
reading.r2: Keep a book in every room you sit in. Boredom becomes the trigger.
reading.hadithText: Read! In the name of your Lord who created.

saving.p3: About {thirtyYear} after 30 years — small money becomes big money.
saving.c1: Skip a year and the gap to retirement widens. Money you didn't save is interest that's working against you instead of for you.
saving.c2: A decade off compound growth is roughly the difference between owning your home outright and renting forever.
saving.r1: Automate it. Move {yearly} a year out of your account before you can spend it — pay yourself first.
saving.r2: Cancel one subscription tonight. Funnel that exact amount into savings.
saving.hadithText: And whoever fears Allah, He will make for him a way out, and provide for him from where he does not expect.

steps.p1: Walking {km} km this year — roughly {laps} times around your neighbourhood.
steps.p2: Over a decade, {decadeKm} km on foot — farther than the long way around the planet.
steps.c1: Skip the walks and you forfeit roughly {km} km of free, easy movement every year.
steps.c2: Sedentary years compound into joint stiffness, lower mood, and a measurably higher risk of depression and back pain.
steps.r1: One daily walk this week — fifteen minutes, no headphones if you can.
steps.r2: Stack it on top of something you already do: a walk after dinner, or to the masjid / shop / friend's place instead of driving.
steps.hadithText: Walking to the mosque is one of the deeds whose reward is multiplied with every step.

exercise.p2: About {km} km on foot — like walking London to Paris {trips, plural, =1 {once} other {# times}}.
exercise.p3: Across a decade, {decadeHours} hours of strength built.
exercise.c1: Sedentary years compound into the metabolic risk profile that fills cardiology wards — diabetes, heart disease, the back pain that never quite goes away.
exercise.c2: Muscle and aerobic capacity you don't build in your 30s are very hard to recover in your 60s.
exercise.r1: Walk 10 minutes today. That's the dose. Tomorrow, the same.
exercise.r2: Tie movement to something you already do — push-ups before every shower, calf raises while the kettle boils.
exercise.hadithText: The strong believer is more beloved to Allah than the weak believer, while there is good in both.

quranPages.p2: You complete the Quran every {monthsPerKhatm} months — {khatmsPerYear, plural, =1 {one khatm} other {# khatmas}} a year.
quranPages.p3: Across a decade, {decadeKhatms} khatmas of revelation.
quranPages.c1: A year of not opening the Mushaf is a year your heart drifts further from the Speech of Allah — quietly, almost imperceptibly.
quranPages.c2: A decade away is a different relationship with the book entirely. The door doesn't close, but the ease of return fades.
quranPages.r1: One page after Fajr. That's the whole strategy.
quranPages.r2: Place the Mushaf on top of your prayer mat tonight. Let it greet you in the morning.
quranPages.hadithText: The best of you are those who learn the Quran and teach it.

screenTime.p1: Stay at {limit} hours a day. Cut one hour and reclaim {daysReclaimed} days a year.
screenTime.p2: Across a decade, {decadeDaysReclaimed} days back in your life.
screenTime.c1: Late-night scrolling shifts melatonin, fragments REM, and bleeds into next morning's focus and patience with the people you love.
screenTime.c2: A decade of comparison feeds — every meal somebody else's, every body somebody else's — and the mind starts believing your life is the small one.
screenTime.r1: Charge the phone outside the bedroom tonight. Buy a cheap alarm clock if you need one.
screenTime.r2: Greyscale the screen, delete the worst app off the home row. Make the slot machine boring.
screenTime.hadithText: From the goodness of a man's Islam is leaving what does not concern him.

tahajjud.p1: {nights} nights of closeness with Allah this year.
tahajjud.p2: {decadeNights} nights of tahajjud across a decade.
tahajjud.c1: Each missed night is a private door of mercy that opened — and closed — without you walking through.
tahajjud.c2: Years of sleeping through the last third of the night quietly remove a kind of intimacy that no daytime worship replaces.
tahajjud.r1: Go to bed earlier tonight. Set the alarm for one rakat — that's enough to begin.
tahajjud.r2: If you wake for water or the bathroom, pray two rakats before lying back down.
tahajjud.hadithText: Our Lord descends to the lowest heaven in the last third of the night and says: Who is calling Me, that I may answer him? Who is asking Me, that I may give him?

sadaqah.p2: {decadeCount} acts across a decade — sadaqah jariyah that keeps giving.
sadaqah.c1: Tightness with money compounds into tightness in the chest. The Sunnah on sadaqah is also a Sunnah on the heart.
sadaqah.c2: Years of holding back when there's room to give — small barakah, small horizon.
sadaqah.r1: Round up your next purchase. Donate the difference, however small.
sadaqah.r2: Set a recurring monthly amount, even if it's tiny. Make giving the default.

learning.p2: About {decadeHours} hours over a decade — enough to master a new field.
learning.c1: A year without learning is a year of repeating yesterday. The market shifts; you stay where you are.
learning.c2: A decade of stagnation is the version of you that gets passed over for the version that kept showing up.
learning.r1: Pick one topic that scares you. Spend 15 minutes on it tonight.
learning.r2: Teach what you learn — a friend, a sibling, a journal entry. Teaching is how it sticks.
learning.hadithText: Whoever travels a path in search of knowledge, Allah makes easy for him a path to Paradise.

gheebat.p1: Hold the tongue. {days} days of clean speech this year.
gheebat.p2: Across a decade, that's {decadeDays} days you protected others' honour.
gheebat.c1: Backbiting is a leak in the same bucket your prayers and fasts are filling. The harder you work, the more painful the leak.
gheebat.c2: A decade of careless speech changes how people relax around you — and how you feel walking into a room.
gheebat.r1: When the urge rises, redirect: say something true and good about the person instead.
gheebat.r2: If you've already wronged someone with words, ask Allah's forgiveness — and where possible, theirs.
gheebat.hadithText: Do you know what backbiting is? It is mentioning your brother in a manner he dislikes.

callFamily.p1: {calls} times a year you reach out — a thread that holds.
callFamily.p2: {decadeCalls} reaching-outs across a decade. People feel them.
callFamily.c1: Parents over 60 have a finite number of weekends left — visits and calls become the most precious currency you have.
callFamily.c2: Children grow up in one direction. The version of them you missed talking to today doesn't return.
callFamily.r1: Call a parent or sibling now. Even if it's awkward. Especially if it's awkward.
callFamily.r2: Schedule a recurring time — Friday after Asr, Sunday morning. Make it a fixed feature, not a feeling.
callFamily.hadithText: Whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day, let him be generous to his guest. Whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day, let him uphold the ties of kinship.
